zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Howard Earl of Suffolk於1561年8月24日出生在英國艾可莎佛朗 瓦爾登時，他的父親Thomas Howard, 4th Duke Of Norfolk25歲，母親Margaret Audley21歲。他與Lady Katherine Knyvett結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1626年5月28日在英國米德萊可倫敦享年64歲，埋葬在英國艾可莎佛朗 瓦爾登。

nameMeanings.name: Howard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: of Norman origin, from the Middle English personal names Huward (also Howard) and Heward, from Old French Huard (itself from ancient Germanic Hugihard, hugi- ‘mind, understanding, spirit’ + hard- ‘hardy, bold’). As Hugh appears in Middle English as both How and Hew, this is the definite origin of Heward and a source of Howard. This surname is also very common among African Americans. See Hugh .

English: from the Middle English personal name Haward or Howard, usually an Anglicized form of Old Danish Hāwarth (Old Norse Hávarthr, from há ‘high’ + varthr ‘guard, guardian, warden’). Alternation between Haward and Howard may have led to later confusion with Hayward .
English: occasionally a variant of   Ewart   2.

variants.name: Fogarty
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, James Patrick, Kieran, Seamus, Siobhan.
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Fógartaigh ‘son of Fógartach’, a personal name from fógartha ‘proclaimed, banished, outlawed’. It is sometimes Anglicized as Howard .

variants.name: Huggard
meanings: English: of Norman origin, from the medieval personal name Hugard, a variant of Huard (see Howard ). This surname is also established in Ireland (Kerry).

variants.name: Hayward
meanings: occupational name from Middle English hayward ‘keeper of the hedges and enclosures’ (Old English hēgweard), a manorial officer in charge of the common fields who protected corn and hay from cattle, theft, or damage, and supervised the harvest.

variants.name: Huard
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Aime, Adelard, Jacques, Jean-Pierre, Lucien, Marcel, Alcide, Amiee, Armand, Aurele, Clermont, Fernard.
French and Channel Islands (Jersey): from the Old French personal name Huart, either identical in origin with Howard 1 or a pet form of Old French Hue .
History: Jean Huard from Courson-Monteloup in Essonne, France, married Anne-Marie Amiot in Quebec City, QC, in 1670.

variants.name: Hugh
meanings: English, Welsh, and Scottish (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Hue, from ancient Germanic Hugo (ultimately derived from hugi- ‘mind, spirit’). This was a very popular name in England after the Norman Conquest, partly due to the reputation of Saint Hugh of Lincoln (1140–1200), who was born in Burgundy and who established the first Carthusian monastery in England.
Scottish and Irish: adopted as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic personal name Aodh, a derivative of a Gaelic word meaning ‘fire’. See Hughes .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 邱 and 丘, based on their Hakka pronunciation; see   Qiu   1 and 2.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Howard Earl of Suffolk was born on 24 August 1561, in Saffron Walden, Essex, England, his father, Thomas Howard, 4th Duke Of Norfolk, was 25 and his mother, Margaret Audley, was 21. He married Lady Katherine Knyvett. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 5 daughters. He died on 28 May 1626, in London, Middlesex, England, at the age of 64, and was buried in Saffron Walden, Essex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Howard Earl of Suffolk est né le 24 août 1561, à Saffron Walden, Essex, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Howard, 4th Duke Of Norfolk, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Margaret Audley, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Lady Katherine Knyvett. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé le 28 mai 1626, à Londres, Middlesex, Angleterre, à l’âge de 64 ans, et a été enterré à Saffron Walden, Essex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas Howard Earl of Suffolk am 24. August 1561 in Saffron Walden, Essex, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas Howard, 4th Duke Of Norfolk 25 und seine Mutter Margaret Audley 21. Er hat Lady Katherine Knyvett geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 7 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Er starb am 28. Mai 1626 im Alter von 64 Jahren in London, Middlesex, England und wurde in Saffron Walden, Essex, England begraben.

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Howard Earl of Suffolk(이)가 1561년 8월 24일 영국 에세스 사프런 월든에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Howard, 4th Duke Of Norfolk(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Audley(은)는 21세였다. 그는 Lady Katherine Knyvett(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1626년 5월 28일 영국 미들레세스 런던에서 64세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 에세스 사프런 월든에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Howard Earl of Suffolk родился 24 августа 1561 г. в местности саффрон валден, эссекс, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas Howard, 4th Duke Of Norfolk было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret Audley, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Katherine Knyvett. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Он умер 28 мая 1626 г. в местности Лондон, миддлесекс, Англия в возрасте 64 лет и был похоронен в местности саффрон валден, эссекс, Англия.
